beat.title: Debating the Mountain Route
beat.boundaryReason: Opening discussion scene at the mountain pass ends when the party starts actively searching for the hidden route.
dateEvidence: Session manifest date is 1748-04-12.
DM opening establishes mid-morning at the mountain pass.
This beat is continuous planning before the search begins.

sourceLines.text: Okay, so

We open tonight on mountains rising from the plains, a sharp boundary between the flat expanses of Dunmar, the plains and deserts that stretch away to the south, and the massive central mountain range that divides the continent.
sourceLines.raw: [u0002 | 00:00:14.130-00:00:28.830 | DM] We open tonight on mountains rising from the plains, a sharp boundary between the flat expanses of Dunmar, the plains and deserts that stretch away to the south, and the massive central mountain range that divides the continent.

sourceLines.text: Worn paths wander through the mountain ranges from the plains below. They jut upward, peaky and snow-capped, against the wildflowers blooming in the spring.
sourceLines.raw: [u0003 | 00:00:33.840-00:00:41.400 | DM] Worn paths wander through the mountain ranges from the plains below. They jut upward, peaky and snow-capped, against the wildflowers blooming in the spring.

sourceLines.text: It's mid-morning, the centaurs having left shortly ago, and you stand looking north at the mountain wall in front of you.
sourceLines.raw: [u0004 | 00:00:42.840-00:00:50.910 | DM] It's mid-morning, the centaurs having left shortly ago, and you stand looking north at the mountain wall in front of you.

sourceLines.text: In the distance you can still see the centaurs riding south towards Karawa, convinced to travel to the Dunmari lands to warn Candrosa.
sourceLines.raw: [u0005 | 00:00:52.890-00:01:00.060 | DM] In the distance you can still see the centaurs riding south towards Karawa, convinced to travel to the Dunmari lands to warn Candrosa.

sourceLines.text: And the rest of the Dunmari about the possible danger of the spreading corruption in the north.
sourceLines.raw: [u0006 | 00:01:01.680-00:01:05.820 | DM] And the rest of the Dunmari about the possible danger of the spreading corruption in the north.

sourceLines.text: The road you're on continues into the mountains north from here.
sourceLines.raw: [u0007 | 00:01:07.650-00:01:10.770 | DM] The road you're on continues into the mountains north from here.

sourceLines.text: A short distance ahead, a flat clearing and camping area stand before the gentle ascent up to the pass.
sourceLines.raw: [u0008 | 00:01:12.300-00:01:18.900 | DM] A short distance ahead, a flat clearing and camping area stand before the gentle ascent up to the pass.

sourceLines.text: To the east, the wall of the mountains continues, but you can see two small streams cascading down from the snowy heights and, a few miles distant along the wall, guarding the approach to the valley of Raven's Hold.
sourceLines.raw: [u0009 | 00:01:19.890-00:01:32.370 | DM] To the east, the wall of the mountains continues, but you can see two small streams cascading down from the snowy heights and, a few miles distant along the wall, guarding the approach to the valley of Raven's Hold.

sourceLines.text: A faint whiff of something rotten and burning carries from the east, despite the stillness and quiet of the plains.
sourceLines.raw: [u0010 | 00:01:33.420-00:01:39.420 | DM] A faint whiff of something rotten and burning carries from the east, despite the stillness and quiet of the plains.

sourceLines.text: But with nothing moving on the wind and an unnatural silence around you, you contemplate your next move.
sourceLines.raw: [u0011 | 00:01:40.650-00:01:46.110 | DM] But with nothing moving on the wind and an unnatural silence around you, you contemplate your next move.

beat.title: Wearing the Roper Down
beat.boundaryReason: Once the party establishes the safe radius, the combat resolves as they kite the roper to death and secure Inakara's promised passage before ending the session.
dateEvidence: Final phase of the same afternoon combat.
The session ends immediately after the roper dies and passage is discussed.
